name: TESTICULAR MICROLITHIASIS

demographics.Etiology: defect in phagocytotic activity of Sertoli cells leaving degenerated intratubular debris
demographics.Histo: laminated concretions within lumen of seminiferous tubules

dominant_finding: may be asymmetrically distributed, unilateral, clustered in periphery
distribution: may be asymmetrically distributed, unilateral, clustered in periphery
discriminating_features: 1-2-mm (> 5) hyperechoic nonshadowing foci scattered throughout the parenchyma of both
may be asymmetrically distributed, unilateral, clustered in periphery

general: 1-2-mm (> 5) hyperechoic nonshadowing foci scattered throughout the parenchyma of both
may be asymmetrically distributed, unilateral, clustered in periphery

clinical.Cx: concurrent germ cell tumor in up to 40% (21.6 x risk)
clinical.Prognosis: the vast majority of patients with testicular microlithiasis will not develop
